heading: Step 1: Construct the policy in the Cedar policy language.
body:
Specify the rules for restricting resources using [Cedar policy language](https://docs.cedarpolicy.com/). You can copy
and modify the following example policies for your organization:

- [Restrict Number of Clusters](restrict-number-of-clusters-api)

- [Restrict Cloud Provider](restrict-cloud-provider)

- [Restrict Cloud Provider Region](restrict-region)

- [Restrict Cloud Provider and Region](example-cedar-policy)

- [Restrict IP Addresses](restrict-ip-addresses)

- [Restrict Cluster Tier Sizes](restrict-cluster-tier)

- [Require Project Maintenance Windows](require-maintenance-window)

- [Prevent Modifications to Network Peering](prevent-peering-modifications)

- [Prevent Modifications to Private Endpoints](prevent-private-endpoint-modifications)

- [Restrict TLS (Transport Layer Security) Protocol and Cipher Suites](restrict-tls-api)

- [Enforce Disk Size (GB)](enforce-disk-size)

- [Enforce Cluster Topology (Replica Set, Sharded, or Global Cluster)](restrict-cluster-type)

- [Enforce Minimum Shard Count](restrict-shard-count)

- [Enforce Dedicated Config Servers for Sharded Clusters](enforce-config-server-management-mode)

> **Note:** We recommend creating multiple, simple Atlas Resource Policies to make tracking easier.
> For example, if you want to restrict cloud provider and multiple regions,
> consider creating one Atlas Resource Policy that restricts the cloud provider and another
> Atlas Resource Policy that restricts the regions.
